rulings.Case Title: VALERIE MARIE BARNES et al VS. RESCARE, INC., A PRIVATE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Demanded At Deposition Of Matt Boyer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 8, Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Demanded At Deposition Of Matt Boyer Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CIAPPONI et al VS. SIMUS, INC. DBA SIMAYOF DIAMOND CUTTERS & JEWELERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT MICKY TAL's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiffs have pled facts sufficient to allege the causes of action for conversion and fraud against Mr. Tal. "One who assumes to act as an agent is responsible to third persons as a principal for his acts in the course of his agency . . . [w]hen his acts are wrongful in their nature." (Civ. Code § 2343(3).) Hearing required. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, LP, A MARYLAND LIMITED et al VS. CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT CASSIDY TURLEY, INC., 's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the existence of the redacted Asset Purchase Agreement, the Court cannot accept the truth of the recitations in that document. Paragraph 5 of the complaint alleges that Capstar and Cassidy Turley merged and the Court must accept the truth of that allegation for purposes of the demurrer. Defendant should bring a motion for summary judgment to resolve this factual issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, LP, A MARYLAND LIMITED et al VS. CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD. and CCRES, LTD.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The complaint sufficiently pleads the discovery rule (19-21) and the allegations do not show that the claims are time barred. The complaint is not so uncertain that defendant cannot reasonably respond. The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of its papers to department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than 4pm February 4, 2013. The Court has the motion and reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANTs DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, DEUTSCHE BANK AG and NICOLE DESANTIS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: JAMES KEENAN et al VS. CALIFORNIA STATE TEACHERS' RETIREMENT SYSTEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Alternative Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013, Line 20, Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Alternative Writ Of Mandate. Hearing required. Counsel are requested to be prepared to address the following questions. 1. Why is CCSF an indispensable party? Respondent manages and controls the pension system under the Education Code. Does the fact that CCSF is pursuing an appeal on behalf of the petitioners change the analysis? 2. Does respondent need to provide petitioners with a meaningful hearing prior to terminating their benefits? (See Petrillo v. Bay Area Rapid Transit District) How does respondent's scheme comport with due process where, as here, the benefits were terminated after the audit proceeding but before the hearing before an ALJ?? 3. What entity made the determination on Keenan's Motion to Postpone Implementation of Decision to Terminate Pension Benefits (Ex. G, Keenan Decl.)? Is Exhibit I (Keenan Declaration) the decision on this motion? 4. Is the fact that petitioner Bloomquist did not file an appeal a difference between his position and that of the other petitioners? 5. Are Petitioners seeking any relief at this point other than the reinstatement of benefits until the administrative appeal before the ALJ is decided? 6. Does the Court need to make a determination regarding whether petitioners engaged in creditable service in order to consider this petition, or is the only issue before the Court whether respondent's scheme, as applied, complies with due process? =(302/MJM)
